title: Jambosa cylindrica
intro: Jambosa cylindrica, also known as the cylindrical jambolan, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ebenaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Jambosa cylindrica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Jambosa cylindrica is often used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Jambosa cylindrica is a species of plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Jambosa, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions. Jambosa cylindrica is further classified into the order Sapindales, which includes other plants like maples and horse chestnuts. The kingdom to which Jambosa cylindrica belongs is Plantae, a group of multicellular, eukaryotic organisms commonly referred to as plants. This species is also classified under the domain Eukarya.
